channel.title: Comments for page "Integrity Project"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Integrity Project"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Getting very heavy Sappho and her friend vibes from this I am assuming Karlyle and Oliver were a bit more than friends

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think it would be very helpful if you looked through the first few paragraphs again. Specifically, because Aktus is referred to as "Aktus", "Karlyle", "the director of Site-81", and "the doctor" in the first few paragraphs, and the narration refers to him as both "Aktus" and "Karlyle" in the third paragraph's first sentence. This was very confusing for me, because it felt like you gave the same character way too many names.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The man, Karlyle, worked with the Foundation to contain 25 SCPs in two years, a record time, then he was smart enough to find patterns in other SCP's behaviors and effects to create more effective containment protocols for them, and he was pretty good at guessing the strength and danger levels of SCPs as well, and he was placed as head of the Classification Committee, whose job it is to classify SCPs as Safe, Euclid, or Keter. Site 81 hasn't had a containment breach ever since Karlyle's work and ideas were implemented in the facility.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To avoid resurrecting a dead comment, I'll just point those wondering about Zena to SCP-2599.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like I don't understand this. At all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hold on, can I have spoilers? Like, a little bit?

+ Please?
- A little help?

So a reality bender is making a ball "green". Could this be SCP-818, SCP-447 or some weird distortion of SCP-239, which I don't think is included yet?

E: well, considering 818 is a boy, maybe this is take 2 on the reality bender recruitment thing?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As I've said before, I'm not really digging "March of the Author Avatars." I actually almost prefer the older, more established author avatars, as they require (at least in theory) far less time to be characterized and set up. This is another long tale with a not-particularly-interesting author avatar who gets propositioned by Danny Ocean peasant farmers the Foundation to rob a casino save their village from bandits do Alpha-9 stuff.

I did really like the ending, though, so no vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I went back and forth on whether to upvote this. In the end I did only because the final scene grabbed my interest enough. But prose-wise this is really shaky.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wait… Kaktus, are you honestly going to circumvent the 90% or so rule by changing the meaning of words?



Brilliant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, yeah. I should comment on that too. I liked this tale quite well overall, but I love the final scene.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aktus is pretty interesting, as author avatars go. That said, I was rather more entranced by his interaction with the girl at the end.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa

Yes, this is Good Town City Hall, County of I Enjoyed It. I am Karl. How may I assist you?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The other man stood. "Twenty-five entities contained within two years. Extensive knowledge of behaviours and anomalous traits of many more. Ability to manipulate situations concerning anomalous entities in order to facilitate containment. Understanding of the—" he paused, "potential, of entities. The reason you joined the Classification Committee. The reason they made you its head. The reason Site 81 has gone without breach in your tenure there."

I… have absolutely no idea what this is supposed to mean. The sentence/paragraph structure is hard enough to parse without having to guess at the backstory.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is actually going to be developed later, but the short of it is that "Jean" is Karlyle's first name. "Karlyle" is his middle name.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why does Joshua insist on calling Aktus "Jean?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's been a busy night!

This is another installment in the Resurrection series, which introduces two additional characters: Site-81 director Karlyle Aktus, and O5 Command Administrative Staff member Joshua.

Many thanks to everybody who looked this over in chat, specifically thedeadlymoose, DrClef, Decibelle, and others.
